seoTitle: How to Make a Resume in Google Docs (2026 Step-by-Step)
metaDescription: Build an ATS-friendly resume in Google Docs using templates or invisible table grids, then fine-tune spacing and export a polished, single-page PDF.
intro: A clean, well-structured resume can make the difference in getting noticed by both hiring managers and applicant tracking systems. This guide shows two approaches in Google Docs: starting from the built-in Template Gallery, or building a custom layout from scratch with a hidden table grid. Either way, you'll end up with a polished, single-page PDF ready to send out.

steps.heading: Start from the Template Gallery
steps.body: Open the Google Docs Template Gallery to browse professionally designed resume layouts, then pick one that fits the role you're applying for.

steps.heading: Or build a custom grid from scratch
steps.body: Use the native Table tool to create a hidden alignment grid, which gives you full control over section placement without visible borders.

steps.heading: Structure your sections with clear headings
steps.body: Apply distinct heading sizes for Professional Summary, Experience, and Education so recruiters can scan your resume quickly.

steps.heading: Fine-tune spacing to fit one page
steps.body: Adjust line-height and cell padding so your content doesn't overflow onto an unnecessary second page.

steps.heading: Hide table borders for a clean look
steps.body: If you used a table grid, set the border weight to zero points or make lines transparent so the underlying structure becomes invisible.

steps.heading: Export as a PDF
steps.body: Save your finished resume as a PDF from the File menu to make sure your layout stays intact no matter what device it's opened on.

faq.question: Are Google Docs resume templates ATS-friendly?
faq.answer: Most built-in templates use standard text formatting that applicant tracking systems can parse, though heavily graphic-based templates may be riskier.

faq.question: How do I keep my resume to one page?
faq.answer: Adjust line spacing, font size, and cell padding incrementally, and trim less relevant bullet points until your content fits cleanly on a single sheet.

faq.question: Should I use a table to structure my resume?
faq.answer: A hidden, borderless table can help align sections cleanly, especially for two-column layouts, without the reader ever seeing a grid.

faq.question: What format should I send my finished resume in?
faq.answer: Exporting to PDF preserves your exact formatting across devices, which is generally the safest format for job applications.
